description_long: This drone features a circular, rigid black frame with four evenly spaced rotors, each housed in a cylindrical casing with blue-tinted tops. The central body is compact and angular, with a combination of metallic gray and white surfaces, and a touch of yellow detailing around the base. The rotors are connected to the main body by sturdy arms, and the overall structure appears robust and engineered for stability. The drone lacks visible landing legs, instead relying on the lower edges of the rotor housings for ground contact. The propeller guards are integrated into the frame, providing protection for the rotors. The design suggests a focus on urban aerial mobility, with a streamlined, modern aesthetic.
description: A circular quadcopter drone with four blue-topped rotors, a compact central body, and integrated propeller guards, designed for urban aerial use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central body with angular details and two rotors prominently in the foreground.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the opposite side of the central body and the rear pair of rotors.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the circular frame and the symmetrical arrangement of all four rotors.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the central body and the two side rotors.
